title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: regional (.e., non-gridded) land use data produced integrated assessment model (use “CoolNewModel” placeholder name ) harmonized downscaled using LUH3 historical high-resolution reference dataset. vignette describes modify mrdownscale process CoolNewModel land data. focuses land variables representing distribution land area across different land use types (e.g. urban, primary forest) cover downscaling non-land variables (e.g. fertilizer), even though mrdownscale capable handling (implemented MAgPIE). questions run trouble, please contact mrdownscale developers (mail address DESCRIPTION file).

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: integrate new model mrdownscale, need create modify five key components. table summarizes step; detailed instructions follow sections .

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: starting, prepare following files: CoolNewModel dataset file - regional land use data format R can read (e.g. CSV) Region mapping file - Defines countries/grid cells belong region Variable mapping file - Maps CoolNewModel variables fine-grained reference variables (see examples inst/extdata/referenceMappings)

what: Install R and configure your environment
title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Install R https://cran.rstudio.com/ Open R find home directory: Create edit file called .Rprofile home directory following content: configuration enables install mrdownscale PIK piam r-universe repository (central CRAN repository) tells madrat store data.
code: normalizePath("~") options(repos = c(runiverse = "https://pik-piam.r-universe.dev",                   CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/")) options(MADRAT_MAINFOLDER = file.path(normalizePath("~"), "madrat_mainfolder"))

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Start fresh R session install required packages:

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: recommend creating fork mrdownscale repository, cloning fork local folder. Apply changes described following sections local folder. test changes, always start fresh R session folder run pkgload::load_all() load modified mrdownscale directly source files. concrete example integrating new model, see PR, integrated COFFEE model mrdownscale.

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: reference mapping file defines CoolNewModel variables map fine-grained reference variables. mapping enables mrdownscale translate CoolNewModel variables supported target dataset format. Create CSV file named coolnewmodel.csv inst/extdata/referenceMappings/. file two columns: CoolNewModel variable names corresponding reference variable names. model cover land categories (example, urban areas included), map missing categories variable called “rest”. ensures total land area remains consistent. ’s example reference mapping COFFEE model: detailed technical information variable mapping, see documentation calcLandInputRecategorized function:
code: cat(readLines(system.file("extdata/referenceMappings/coffee.csv",                           package = "mrdownscale"), n = 30), sep = "\n") #> reference;data #> primf;Land_Cover_Forest_Primary #> secdf;Land_Cover_Forest_Secondary #> forestry_ndc;Land_Cover_Forest_Secondary #> forestry_aff_secdf;Land_Cover_Forest_Secondary #> forestry_aff_pltns;Land_Cover_Forest_Planted #> forestry_plant;Land_Cover_Forest_Planted #> primn;Land_Cover_Other_Natural #> secdn;Land_Cover_Other_Natural #> pastr;Land_Cover_Pasture #> range;Land_Cover_Pasture #> urban;rest #> c3per_irrigated_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c3per_rainfed_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c4per_irrigated_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c4per_rainfed_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c3ann_irrigated_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c3ann_rainfed_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c4ann_irrigated_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c4ann_rainfed_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c3nfx_irrigated_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> c3nfx_rainfed_biofuel_1st_gen;rest #> begr, irrigated;Land_Cover_Cropland_Grassy #> begr, rainfed;Land_Cover_Cropland_Grassy #> betr, irrigated;Land_Cover_Cropland_Woody #> betr, rainfed;Land_Cover_Cropland_Woody #> alfalfa, irrigated;rest #> alfalfa, rainfed;rest #> Almonds, with shell, irrigated;Land_Cover_Cropland_Nuts #> Almonds, with shell, rainfed;Land_Cover_Cropland_Nuts ?mrdownscale:::calcLandInputRecategorized

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: mrdownscale uses madrat framework handle data loading. madrat framework automatically manages file organization, caching, reproducibility. reading data, must set file structure create read function.

previous_headings: Integration Steps > Step 2: Create Read Function
what: Set up the data folder structure
title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: First, check madrat stores source data: folder, create new subfolder called CoolNewModel place data files region mapping file .

previous_headings: Integration Steps > Step 2: Create Read Function
title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Write function named readCoolNewModel new file R/readCoolNewModel.R. function must read two different data types, specified via subtype parameter: subtype = "data" - reads land use data returns data frame subtype = "resolutionMapping" - reads region mapping file returns data frame ’s example read function use template: Important: function name must match source folder name exactly (“read” prefix). “CoolNewModel” folder, name readCoolNewModel.
code: mrdownscale:::readWITCH #> function (subtype = "data")  #> { #>     if (subtype == "data") { #>         x <- utils::read.csv("db_ssp2_M_luh3.csv") #>         return(list(x = x, class = "data.frame", unit = paste(unique(x$units),  #>             collapse = ", "), description = "WITCH data")) #>     } #>     else if (subtype == "resolutionMapping") { #>         mapping <- utils::read.csv("resolution_mapping_witch.csv") #>         regions <- unique(mapping$witch17) #>         mapping <- mapping[, setdiff(colnames(mapping), "lowRes")] #>         addId <- data.frame(witch17 = regions, lowRes = paste0(regions,  #>             ".", seq_along(regions))) #>         mapping <- merge(mapping, addId, "witch17") #>         return(list(x = mapping, class = "data.frame", description = "WITCH resolution mapping")) #>     } #>     else { #>         stop("Unexpected subtype, only data and resolutionMapping are accepted") #>     } #> } #> <bytecode: 0x563ad380a6f0> #> <environment: namespace:mrdownscale>

previous_headings: Integration Steps > Step 2: Create Read Function
title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Start fresh R session verify setup works: Note readCoolNewModel never called directly. Instead, use readSource(), handles changing correct folder, caching results, loading cached data nothing changed.
code: pkgload::load_all("path/to/mrdownscale") x <- readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "data") print(x) map <- readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "resolutionMapping") print(map)

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Add conditional branch model R/calcLandInput.R: section, apply following consistency checks transformations ensure data meets mrdownscale’s requirements: missing values - Ensure NA NaN values data Non-negative values - variables must >= 0 Non-overlapping categories - Variables overlap (e.g. avoid “forest” “primary forest” separate categories sum total forest area) Constant total area time - sum variables remain constant across years. needed, add “rest” category account missing land use types Convert magpie format - Use .magpie(x) convert data magpie object format Match variable names - Ensure variable names exactly match reference mapping file
code: if (input == "magpie") {   # existing code ... } else if (input == "CoolNewModel") {   x <- readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "data")   # apply validations here (see below) }

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Add conditional branch model R/calcResolutionMapping.R: function must create mapping regional level target grid level. country--region mapping, can use existing magpie mapping, includes country--grid mappings. done COFFEE, see input == "coffee" -branch R/calcResolutionMapping.R.
code: if (input == "magpie") {   # existing code ... } else if (input == "CoolNewModel") {   x <- readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "resolutionMapping")   # create mapping to target grid here }

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: Add reference mapping file tool function R/toolLandCategoriesMapping.R: Place variable mapping CSV file inst/extdata/referenceMappings/ (created Step 1) Add new condition -else statement toolLandCategoriesMapping.R returns mapping model, following pattern used models

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: integration steps complete, can calculate harmonized data model’s native regional resolution: produces CSV file containing data harmonized LUH3 model’s regional resolution. harmonization period specifies time range harmonize, harmonization method determines model data modified smoothly continue historical observations.
code: harmonized <- calcOutput("LandHarmonized",                          input = "CoolNewModel",                          target = "luh3",                          harmonizationPeriod = c(2025, 2050),                          harmonization = "fadeForest") write.magpie(harmonized, "harmonized.csv")

what: Running the Full Pipeline - Harmonization and Downscaling
title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: run complete pipeline including harmonization downscaling grid resolution: completes successfully, working directory contain NetCDF files (.nc) harmonized downscaled land use data grid resolution.

title: How to downscale data from a new model
text: running harmonization downscaling, verify completed following: Created reference mapping file: inst/extdata/referenceMappings/coolnewmodel.csv Created read function: R/readCoolNewModel.R (reads “data” “resolutionMapping” subtypes) Tested read function readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "data") readSource("CoolNewModel", subtype = "resolutionMapping") Added branch R/calcLandInput.R transformations, consistency checks pass Data missing values, values >= 0, non-overlapping categories, matches reference mapping variable names Added branch R/calcResolutionMapping.R map regions grid cells Added condition R/toolLandCategoriesMapping.R register reference mapping

what: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: REMIND-MAgPIE runs used downscaled land use dataset described corresponding downscaled emissions dataset. Find changelog general setup including model versions settings, well emissions dataset https://github.com/IAMconsortium/concordia/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md.

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: changed harmonization interpolation function, mostly reduces difference end harmonization replace fulwd/rndwd NAs country shares instead 0.5 consistency check results now written logfile calculate low high resolution mapping (fill missing nearest neighbor) data now matches land/sea mask LUH2v2h/CMIP6 fixed wood harvest share cell area fixed bug total areas land type match downscaling fixed: primf/primn expansion longer happening LUH data wood harvest fertilizer now aggregated n years match input data semantics fixed bug wrong reference year used downscaling wood harvest area now consistent land nonland/management data now downscaled using already downscaled land data reference

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: use yearly LUH data 1995-2015 extrapolation base year 2015 now included reported data

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: fixed flipped latitude removed bounds_time report data 2015 2100 (opposed 1995) added units management transitions nc files

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: fixed wood harvest area (unit) conversion bug removed NAs renamed longitude/latitude lon/lat added bounds variables lon/lat/time added metadata, incl. harmonization period

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: dataset 1995-2100 5-year timesteps REMIND-MAgPIE scenarios used emissions added metadata nc files management.nc: added fulwd, rndwd transitions.nc: reporting LUH variables incl. wood harvest variables transitions gross transitions transitions 2100 net-zero gross transitions (used extended (2100-2300) scenarios) cover LUH cells (e.g. Greenland missing )

title: Land Use Data for ESMs - Changelog
text: dataset 1995-2100 5-year timesteps one scenario states.nc: reporting LUH variables except secma, secmb management.nc: reporting LUH variables except fulwd, rndwd, combf, flood, fharv_c3per, fharv_c4per additionally reporting 2nd gen biofuel (crpbf2_c3per, crpbf2_c4per) managed forests (manaf) management.nc

title: calcLandInput — calcLandInput
text: Prepare land input data category mapping, checking data consistency returning. "Land" functions deal area data, opposed "Nonland" functions deal non-area data amount applied fertilizer. treated differently, area data constraints apply, e.g. total area must constant time.

title: calcLandInput — calcLandInput
text: input name input dataset, options: "magpie", "witch"

title: calcLandInput — calcLandInput
text: land input data

title: calcLandInput — calcLandInput
text: input = "magpie": includes land use categories past (pasture, including rangeland), forestry (managed forest plantations), primforest, secdforest, urban, (land) many specific crop types. Furthermore, 1st gen biofuel added filled zeros. 1st gen biofuel modeled implicitly magpie via demand, trade unclear area 1st gen biofuel grown, also 1st gen biofuel quickly phased magpie, fill biofuel_1st_gen zeros rely harmonization produce plausible 1st gen biofuel time series. input = "witch": includes subset LUH land use categories: primf, primn, secdn, pastr, c4per, pltns, secdf, c3ann_irrigated, c3ann_rainfed given shares. "rest" category added shares sum 1.

title: calcLandInput — calcLandInput
text: Jan Philipp Dietrich, Pascal Sauer

Thereby new source new target can supported supplying map new input /target reference categories. input = "witch": "rest" category added calcLandInput disaggregated missing land variables using reference dataset included mrdownscale. Thus following variables added, part input scenario data: range, urban, c4ann_*, c3per_*, c3nfx_* allows harmonization downscaling pipeline continue complete dataset, variables used reported (least checked extensively).
